categories.name: Food & Drink
items: You've eaten waakye with shito and everything on top
You know the jollof rice vs. Nigeria argument by heart
You've eaten banku and okro soup with your hands
You've chewed sugarcane till your jaw hurt
Kelewele is your go-to late night snack
You've fought over the last piece of meat in the soup
You've drunk pure water from a sachet
You know 'tea' means Milo, not actual tea
You've had kenkey with fried fish and pepper
You've eaten fufu and wished you had more soup
You've had 'check check' (instant fried rice) as a full meal
You know the pain of biting into a hot pepper in your stew
You've bought roasted plantain (kofi brokeman) from the roadside
You've bought more than one egg and pepper (kosua ne mako) at a go
You've snacked on aburo ne nkateε (graphic and times) from your pockets

categories.name: Transport & Getting Around
items: You've heard a trotro mate yell 'Circle! Circle!'
You've experienced 'my change' drama on a trotro
You've squeezed into a trotro seat meant for 3 with 5 people
You've been stuck in Accra traffic for 2+ hours
Your Uber driver called you 3 times for directions
You've ridden an okada to beat traffic
You've ridden Pragya because you could not get trotro
You know 'dropping' is not about falling
You've experienced the Kasoa-Accra traffic on a Monday morning
You've taken a VIP bus to Kumasi or Cape Coast
You've stopped over at Linda Dor en route to Kumasi
You've been beaten by rain while sitting inside a taxi or troski

categories.name: Diaspora Life
items: Someone asked you to bring 'something small' from abroad
You've packed a barrel or box to send home
You've been told 'you've changed' when you visit
You've had to explain where Ghana is to a foreigner
You pronounce words differently depending on who you're with
You have an auntie or uncle who is not actually related to you
You've been guilt-tripped for not calling home enough
You've said 'I'm coming' and showed up 2 hours later
You've converted prices to cedis and gasped
Your suitcase is 70% stuff for other people when traveling home
You've been asked to sponsor a visa for someone you barely know
You still overuse 'please' in your sentences

categories.name: Social Media & Pop Culture
items: You've used 'eiii' as a complete sentence
You've said 'chale' at least 5 times in one conversation
You've argued about who the best Ghanaian artist is
You know every word to at least one Daddy Lumba song
You've laughed till you cried watching a Kumawood movie
You've shared an Akrobeto news clip
You've used 'as for you' to start a sentence
You add 'ooo' to the end of sentences for emphasis
You've used 'chale' in a WhatsApp voice note
You've defended Ghana Twitter in an online argument
You watch KSS for your daily news
You watch Headless YouTuber for your daily news
You follow the 'ghanatwitter' IG page

categories.name: Family & Home Life
items: Your parents have threatened you with a slipper
You've been sent to buy something mid-conversation
You've heard 'when I was your age...' at least 100 times
You've attended a funeral that felt like a party
An older person has called you by your day name
You know your day name (Kwame, Ama, Yaa, etc.)
You've been forced to greet every single person in a room
Your family WhatsApp group is absolute chaos
You've been asked 'so when are you getting married?' at every gathering
You've eaten at a family event and been told to take food home too
Your mum has used 'You want to kill me' to guilt trip you
A family member has played cupid and tried to set you up with somebody

categories.name: Music & Entertainment
items: You can do at least one azonto move
You've danced to Ofori Amponsah's 'Otoolege'
Highlife music hits different at family gatherings
You've been to a church service that lasted 4+ hours
The Black Stars have broken your heart at least once
You've watched TV3 Date Rush
You've argued GH music vs. Naija music
You know at least three Sarkodie bars by heart
You've stayed up late to watch a Black Stars match
You have highlife in your everyday music playlist

categories.name: Everyday Struggles & Realities
items: Dumsor has ruined your plans at least once
You've charged your phone at someone else's house
You've used a flashlight or candle during a blackout
You've bargained a price down at Makola or Kantamanto
You've been woken up by a rooster or church speakers
You've experienced 'Ghana time' - nothing starts on time
Network issues have made you switch SIM cards
You've had to 'flash' someone because you had no credit
You've experienced water rationing and had to store water in buckets
You've heard 'the system is down' at a government office
You've swapped a phone at Circle instead of buying a new one

categories.name: Patriotism & National Pride
items: You get emotional hearing the national anthem
You proudly say 'I'm Ghanaian' to anyone who asks
March 6 means something special to you
You know the words to the national pledge
You know the second stanza of the national anthem
You know the third stanza of the national anthem
You've worn kente or African print with pride
You feel personally offended when someone mistakes Ghana for another country
You've repped the Black Star in some form - jersey, flag, sticker
You own a batakari or fugu

freeSquareText: You are Ghanaian (or wish you were)
